name: Ransomware Mass File Encryption - High-Entropy Extension Appended to Data Files
description: Detects a process appending a custom extension to common data file types, which is the write phase of the ransomware encrypt-then-replace cycle. Any process that creates files matching <original>.<ransomware_ext> at high volume across user directories is exhibiting core T1486 behavior regardless of tooling. Covers documented extensions from Conti, LockBit, BlackCat/ALPHV, Ryuk, REvil, WannaCry, and similar families.
query:
file where event.action == "creation" and
  (
    file.path : ("*.encrypted", "*.locked", "*.crypted", "*.enc", "*.crypt",
                  "*.ransom", "*.WNCRY", "*.ryk", "*.lck", "*.darkness",
                  "*.breaking_bad", "*.cryptowall", "*.cryptolocker")
  ) and
  not process.name : ("MsMpEng.exe", "svchost.exe", "SearchIndexer.exe")
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Access Token Manipulation - OpenProcess on SYSTEM-Context Processes
description: Detects a non-system process opening a handle to winlogon.exe or lsass.exe with query or duplicate-handle access rights. Ransomware and privilege-escalation malware enumerate processes via CreateToolhelp32Snapshot then call OpenProcess on SYSTEM processes to steal tokens (T1134.001). The combination of a user-space source image querying lsass.exe or winlogon.exe with non-trivial access rights is high-confidence malicious.
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Ransom Note Deployment - Known Ransom Note Filenames Written to User Directories
description: Detects file-creation events where the filename matches well-known ransom note naming conventions used by real-world ransomware families. Ransom notes are dropped to every encrypted directory as well as Desktop, Documents, and the user profile root. A single occurrence of any of these filenames in a user-accessible path is a critical indicator. Covers naming patterns from Conti, LockBit 2.0/3.0, BlackCat/ALPHV, Ryuk, REvil, Maze, Hive, and common generic patterns.
query:
file where event.action == "creation" and
  (
    file.name : (
      "README_RANSOMWARE.txt",
      "YOUR_FILES_ENCRYPTED.html",
      "PAYMENT_INSTRUCTIONS.txt",
      "HOW_TO_DECRYPT.txt",
      "DECRYPT_INSTRUCTIONS.txt",
      "RECOVER_FILES.txt",
      "FILES_ENCRYPTED.txt",
      "!README!.txt",
      "RESTORE_FILES.txt",
      "CONTI_README.txt",
      "ReadMe.txt",
      "LOCKBIT_README.txt",
      "!!!-Restore-My-Files-!!!",
      "HELPME.txt",
      "DECRYPT.txt"
    ) or
    file.name : ("*DECRYPT*", "*ENCRYPTED*", "*RANSOM*", "*RESTORE_FILES*")
  ) and
  (
    file.path : ("C:\\Users\\*", "C:\\ProgramData\\*")
  )
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Ransomware Killchain Sequence - Token Manipulation Followed by Encrypted File Creation
description: Sequence detection correlating privilege escalation (process access on lsass.exe or winlogon.exe) within the same host followed by file creation events bearing ransomware extensions within a 5-minute window. This multi-step correlation dramatically reduces false positives - a single host performing both token manipulation and mass file encryption in rapid succession is a near-certain ransomware execution. Modeled on documented Conti, LockBit, and BlackCat behavior.
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Ransomware Desktop Wallpaper Modification - Registry Write by Unexpected Process
description: Detects writes to HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop\Wallpaper or WallpaperStyle registry values by processes other than known Windows UI components. Ransomware families including BlackCat/ALPHV, REvil/Sodinokibi, and Ryuk modify the desktop wallpaper to display a ransom demand image. Writing a non-standard path to the Wallpaper value from a non-UI process is a strong indicator of T1491.001 defacement behavior.
query:
registry where event.action == "modification" and
  registry.path : (
    "HKEY_USERS\\*\\Control Panel\\Desktop\\Wallpaper",
    "HKEY_USERS\\*\\Control Panel\\Desktop\\WallpaperStyle",
    "HKU\\*\\Control Panel\\Desktop\\Wallpaper",
    "HKU\\*\\Control Panel\\Desktop\\WallpaperStyle"
  ) and
  not process.name : (
    "explorer.exe",
    "SystemSettings.exe",
    "svchost.exe",
    "UserOOBEBroker.exe",
    "rundll32.exe"
  )
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator
